stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Meta-Llama-3-8B
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of Meta-Llama-3-8B on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key AI/ML domains including 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
The presence of 'safetensors' indicates modern, secure model handling practices.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: meta-llama/Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the trending status of the Llama-3.1-8B-Instruct model on Hugging Face.
It is categorized under key ML/NLP tags like 'transformers' and 'text-generation'.
This indicates active community interest and benchmarking around Meta's Llama ecosystem.

stories.title: Google's SynthID AI watermarking tech is being adopted by OpenAI, Nvidia, and more
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google's SynthID watermarking technology is gaining traction across the AI industry.
Major players like OpenAI and Nvidia are adopting SynthID to combat AI-generated misinformation.
The technology addresses the growing challenge of distinguishing authentic content from synthetic AI output.

stories.title: In stunning display of stupid, secret CISA credentials found in public GitHub repo
stories.author: Lee Hutchinson
bullet_points: Sensitive credentials (SSH keys, plaintext passwords) were exposed in a public GitHub repository.
The exposed data included CISA credentials, indicating a high-profile security risk.
The vulnerability has been present in the public repository since November 2025.

stories.title: Gemini 3.5 Flash
stories.author: spectraldrift
bullet_points: Announcement of Gemini 3.5 Flash, indicating a new iteration in Google's AI model lineup.
The release suggests a focus on efficiency and speed, typical of 'Flash' model designations.
The news is directly relevant to developers and the broader AI industry due to its API availability.

stories.title: OpenAI Adopts Google's SynthID Watermark for AI Images with Verification Tool
stories.author: smooke
bullet_points: OpenAI is integrating Google's SynthID technology into its AI image generation process.
This adoption signifies a major industry move toward digital provenance and watermarking for synthetic media.
The inclusion of a verification tool addresses growing concerns over AI-generated content authenticity.

stories.title: Show HN: Forge – Guardrails take an 8B model from 53% to 99% on agentic tasks
stories.author: zambelli
bullet_points: Forge is an open-source reliability layer designed for self-hosted LLM tool-calling.
It implements domain-agnostic guardrails (e.g., retry nudges, error recovery) to improve LLM performance.
The system significantly boosts an 8B model's success rate on multi-step agentic tasks from 53% to 99% without retraining the model.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The news highlights the trending status of the 'deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-R1' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
The model is categorized under text-generation and conversational AI pipelines.
It utilizes standard ML components like transformers and safetensors, indicating a focus on practical LLM deployment.

stories.title: Ollama is now powered by MLX on Apple Silicon in preview
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama, a popular local LLM runner, now integrates MLX for enhanced performance.
This integration specifically targets Apple Silicon, optimizing AI workloads on Apple hardware.
The feature is currently available in a preview build, indicating ongoing development and optimization.

stories.title: OpenClaw
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: OpenClaw is a personal AI assistant designed to integrate with messaging applications.
It utilizes local AI coding agents, emphasizing on-device processing for privacy and control.
The tool aims to bridge the gap between messaging platforms and local, private AI capabilities.

stories.title: ollama launch
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama introduces a new command for running coding tools locally or in the cloud.
It simplifies the setup process by requiring no environment variables or config files.
The tool supports various coding models, including Claude Code, OpenCode, and Codex.

stories.title: OpenAI gpt-oss-safeguard
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama is integrating OpenAI's gpt-oss-safeguard reasoning models for safety classification tasks.
The models come in two sizes (20B and 120B) and are released under the permissive Apache 2.0 license.
The partnership involves Ollama, OpenAI, and ROOST (Robust Open Online Safety Tools).

stories.title: Demis Hassabis said this might be the ‘foothills of the singularity.’ What?
stories.author: Stevie Bonifield
bullet_points: Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis made a high-profile statement at Google I/O regarding AGI.
Hassabis framed current advancements as potentially being the 'foothills of the singularity,' indicating major future potential.
The statement emphasized that Google's research aims to unlock AGI's potential for global benefit.

stories.title: The future of Google is a search box that does everything
stories.author: Jay Peters
bullet_points: Google's strategic shift is moving beyond simple search functionality.
The focus is on developing a unified interface where the search box handles comprehensive tasks.
This indicates a major industry trend toward AI-driven, all-in-one digital assistants.

stories.title: Two AI-based science assistants succeed with drug-retargeting tasks
stories.author: John Timmer
bullet_points: Two AI-based science assistants have demonstrated success in drug-retargeting tasks.
The tools are capable of generating scientific hypotheses, indicating advanced reasoning capabilities.
One of the assistants further demonstrated capability by analyzing relevant data.

stories.title: Gemini 3.5 Flash might be fast enough for gen AI to make sense
stories.author: Ryan Whitwam
bullet_points: Google is positioning Gemini 3.5 Flash as a crucial component for the future of agentic AI.
The focus of the announcement is on the model's efficiency, suggesting it balances performance with speed.
The development implies a push toward making generative AI applications more practical and deployable.

stories.title: EngiAI: A Multi-Agent Framework and Benchmark Suite for LLM-Driven Engineering Design
stories.author: Mark Fuge
bullet_points: Introduces EngiAI, a new multi-agent framework specifically designed for LLM-driven engineering design.
Addresses a gap in current research by providing a benchmark suite that evaluates complex multi-agent systems combining simulation, retrieval, and manufacturing prep.
The benchmark suite includes a workflow evaluation with seven prompt styles targeting diverse cognitive demands like tool use and conditional branching.

stories.title: Awakening the Hydra: Stabilizing Multi-Concept Backdoor Injection in Text-to-Image Diffusion Models
stories.author: Songze Li
bullet_points: The research addresses the vulnerability of text-to-image diffusion models to multi-concept backdoor injection.
The risk is amplified in open-source ecosystems where models undergo sequential fine-tuning by multiple parties.
The core problem involves the accumulation of conflicting concept-specific trigger-target associations within a single model.

stories.title: Gemini 3.5 Flash Developer Guide
stories.author: pat_loeber
bullet_points: Gemini 3.5 Flash is now generally available (GA).
The model is reported to be stable and production-ready.
The content serves as a developer guide, indicating practical application readiness.

stories.title: 🤗 Trending: bigscience/bloom
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: The story highlights the 'bigscience/bloom' model on Hugging Face Benchmarks.
It is categorized under key NLP/ML tags like 'transformers' and 'pytorch'.
The pipeline demonstrated is 'text-generation', indicating a generative AI focus.

stories.title: Claude Code with Anthropic API compatibility
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama now supports the Anthropic Messages API.
This integration allows users to leverage Claude Code functionality within Ollama.
It facilitates the use of advanced models like Claude Code alongside open-source models.

stories.title: OpenAI Codex with Ollama
stories.author: unknown
bullet_points: Ollama enables the use of OpenAI's Codex CLI with open-source models.
Codex functionality allows models to read, modify, and execute code within a working directory.
The integration supports various open-weight alternatives, such as gpt-oss:20b and gpt-oss:120b.

stories.title: Google’s AI future demands trust — and your personal data
stories.author: Emma Roth
bullet_points: Google is heavily emphasizing the need for user trust in its future AI ecosystem.
New AI tools like Gemini Spark and Daily Brief were showcased at I/O 2026.
These tools aim to integrate deeply into daily life, offering organizational and informational assistance.

stories.title: FBI seeks US-wide access to license plate cameras, wants "data in near real time"
stories.author: Jon Brodkin
bullet_points: The FBI is seeking nationwide access to license plate camera data.
The request involves paying vendors to facilitate tracking and searching of vehicles across the US.
A key demand is for this data to be available to the FBI in 'near real time'.

stories.title: "I'll buy 10 of those"—NASA science chief yearns for mass-produced satellites
stories.author: Stephen Clark
bullet_points: NASA's science chief is advocating for the mass production of satellites to increase scientific data collection in space.
The initiative addresses the challenge of limited access to space for scientific missions.
This points toward a potential industry shift towards more accessible and scalable space infrastructure.

stories.title: Prior Knowledge or Search? A Study of LLM Agents in Hardware-Aware Code Optimization
stories.author: Egor Shvetsov
bullet_points: The study investigates LLM agents used in hardware-aware code optimization, focusing on the propose-evaluate-revise loop.
It addresses the challenge of understanding which components of complex LLM agents contribute most to optimization success or failure.
The research utilizes three controlled experiments to analyze the interplay between prior knowledge and search mechanisms in LLM agents.

stories.title: Distribution-Free Uncertainty Quantification for Continuous AI Agent Evaluation
stories.author: Yi Ling Yu
bullet_points: Introduces distribution-free uncertainty quantification methods (split conformal prediction and ACI) for evaluating continuous AI agents.
Achieves high calibration accuracy (error < 0.02) for forecasted quality scores over a 24h horizon.
Extends the methodology to provide compositional uncertainty bounds for complex, multi-agent pipelines.

stories.title: B-cos GNNs: Faithful Explanations through Dynamic Linearity
stories.author: Verena Wolf
bullet_points: Introduces B-cos GNNs, a new class of inherently explainable Graph Neural Networks.
The model achieves exact decomposition of predictions into per-node and per-feature contributions via a single linear map.
It replaces traditional non-linear message/update functions with B-cos transforms to induce task-specific weight-input alignment.

stories.title: AR1-ZO: Topology-Aware Rank-1 Zeroth-Order Queries for High-Rank LoRA Fine-Tuning
stories.author: Yao Shu
bullet_points: The research addresses a 'rank paradox' when combining Zeroth-Order (ZO) optimization with LoRA for LLM fine-tuning.
ZO optimization allows LLM fine-tuning without storing backpropagation activations, offering memory efficiency.
The paper proposes a topology-aware approach to overcome measurement issues that limit the effectiveness of standard ZO methods with high-rank LoRA adapters.

stories.title: MSAlign: Aligning Molecule and Mass Spectra Foundation Models for Metabolite Identification
stories.author: Florence d'Alché-Buc
bullet_points: The research addresses the critical challenge of metabolite identification from mass spectrometry (MS) data in metabolomics.
It introduces MSAlign, a foundation model designed to align molecule and mass spectra for metabolite retrieval.
The work leverages recent advancements in benchmark datasets like MassSpecGym and Spectraverse to improve accuracy.

stories.title: Google changes its search box
stories.author: berkeleyjunk
bullet_points: Google is reportedly integrating AI (Gemini) into its search bar.
This represents a significant shift in how users interact with search engines.
The news is being discussed across major tech and business publications.

stories.title: Mistral AI acquires Emmi AI
stories.author: doener
bullet_points: Mistral AI, a prominent AI company, has acquired Emmi AI.
This acquisition suggests a strategic move by Mistral to expand its capabilities or market reach.
The news is relevant to the competitive landscape of the generative AI industry.

stories.title: How we used Quint to find over 10 bugs in SQLite while hardening Turso
stories.author: turso.tech via typesanitizer
bullet_points: The article details the use of a tool named Quint for security auditing.
The audit successfully identified over 10 bugs within the SQLite database engine.
The work was conducted as part of hardening the Turso database service.

stories.title: AI isn't replacing junior devs. Your org chart is.
stories.author: dennistraub
bullet_points: The article reframes the debate on AI replacing junior developers by separating individual capability from organizational structure.
The core argument suggests that the true risk associated with AI adoption is flawed pipeline decision-making, not individual job replacement.
It posits that organizational design, rather than individual skill, is the primary factor influencing the impact of AI on junior developer roles.

stories.title: Google AI Edge Gallery Now Runs MCP On-Device. The Privacy Architecture
stories.author: om_shree_0709
bullet_points: Google AI Edge Gallery now supports running MCP (likely a specific AI model or framework) directly on-device.
The article focuses on the privacy architecture enabled by running AI models locally.
The content is presented as a submission for the Google I/O Writing Challenge.
